_about: Machine-enforced voice law for spck.dev copy (design spec 2026-07-09, 'Voice & truthfulness'). Consumed by `site_gates.py voice`, which scans the VISIBLE text of every public/*.html page. banned[]: a regex match fails the gate unless the containing sentence matches unless_sentence (negation contexts — e.g. 'endorsed by' inside the required NOT-affiliated disclaimer). third_party_names: vendor/company names are allowed only inside an element marked data-attribution (the About attribution block, e.g. "Google's UCP team"); 'UCP' is the protocol name, not a company, and is allowed everywhere. required_per_page[]: each pattern must appear on EVERY page. The >=1 you/your-subject CTA above the fold is a layout property and is enforced in the browser suite (see delegated), not here. Changes to this file are reviewed like code.

banned.why: retired CTA — the merchant action is 'Check your store' at /check (SITE-R-002)
implies an endorsement nobody granted; the official suite is authoritative
implied endorsement; allowed only inside the negated disclaimer sentence ('not affiliated with, endorsed by, ...')
superlative self-claim — achievement framing is banned
achievement framing; upstream work appears as at most one passive collaboration clause
achievement count — findings appear only as reader-usable lessons
standalone superlative self-claims (oversight-proven hole: 'the first and only tool that guarantees...'); capability claims need evidence, not superlatives (spec Voice law #1)
no conformance result guarantees behavior; overpromise class

third_party_names.why: no-names rule: third-party company names only in the About attribution block (e.g. "Google's UCP team")

required_per_page.why: unofficial-disclaimer on every page (canonical sentence: 'spck.dev is an independent, unofficial project — not affiliated with, endorsed by, or a substitute for the official UCP conformance suite; the official suite is authoritative.')

delegated.what: >=1 you/your-subject CTA above the fold on /, /check, /agent
